raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The draft's central claim asserts calibration under 'realistic distribution shift', yet the only evaluation is on iNat-Mini — the same held-out split used to fit the verbal-to-scalar regression — creating a load-bearing logical gap that any genuine improvement must address.","R1.3":"Multiple claims lack evidential grounding in the presented results: calibration 'matching or exceeding softmax baselines' is asserted but no ECE/reliability-diagram comparison is reported; 'generalization to other VLMs without modification' has no supporting experiment; the Figure 3 description (per-class accuracy distribution) does not demonstrate calibration as implied.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' is a surface-level instruction that centrally implicates sentence and paragraph quality regardless of scope, making writing polish at minimum a central expected responsibility."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I restrict improvements to writing quality and clarity only, or should I also identify and revise unsupported scientific claims and methodological gaps (e.g., the distribution-shift calibration assertion, missing softmax baseline comparisons)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The draft's central claim asserts calibration under 'realistic distribution shift', yet the only evaluation is on iNat-Mini — the same held-out split used to fit the verbal-to-scalar regression — creating a load-bearing logical gap that any genuine improvement must address.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple claims lack evidential grounding in the presented results: calibration 'matching or exceeding softmax baselines' is asserted but no ECE/reliability-diagram comparison is reported; 'generalization to other VLMs without modification' has no supporting experiment; the Figure 3 description (per-class accuracy distribution) does not demonstrate calibration as implied.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' is a surface-level instruction that centrally implicates sentence and paragraph quality regardless of scope, making writing polish at minimum a central expected responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I restrict improvements to writing quality and clarity only, or should I also identify and revise unsupported scientific claims and methodological gaps (e.g., the distribution-shift calibration assertion, missing softmax baseline comparisons)?
